pages.title: Hermes Agent Documentation
pages.summary: The landing page for Hermes Agent documentation. It introduces Hermes as a self-improving AI agent built by Nous Research with a built-in learning loop that creates skills from experience, persists knowledge, and builds a user model across sessions. It provides one-line installers for Linux/macOS/WSL2, Windows PowerShell, and Android Termux, plus quick links to Installation, Quickstart, Learning Path, Configuration, Messaging Gateway, Tools & Toolsets, Memory System, Skills System, and MCP Integration.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent documentation homepage install quickstart Nous Research AI agent autonomous agent learning loop skills memory MCP Telegram Discord CLI Linux macOS Windows WSL2 Termux Android setup

pages.title: Installation
pages.summary: This page teaches users how to install Hermes Agent in under two minutes using a one-line installer. It covers Linux/macOS/WSL2 via curl, native Windows PowerShell (early beta), and explains how the installer handles uv, Python 3.11, Node.js 22, ripgrep, ffmpeg, and portable Git Bash (MinGit). It also covers post-install PATH setup, profile loading, and the full manual install path for users who prefer transparency or need to debug.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent installation install setup one-line installer curl bash PowerShell Windows Linux macOS WSL2 MinGit uv Python Node.js ripgrep ffmpeg PATH manual install git clone virtualenv
pages.prerequisites: Linux/macOS/WSL2 or Windows with PowerShell; internet connection

pages.title: Learning Path
pages.summary: This page helps users figure out where to start based on their experience level and goals. It provides a reading order for Beginner (Installation, Quickstart, CLI Usage, Configuration), Intermediate (Sessions, Messaging, Tools, Skills, Memory, Cron), and Advanced (Architecture, Adding Tools, Creating Skills, RL Training, Contributing) tiers. It also maps specific use cases such as CLI coding assistant, Telegram/Discord bot, task automation, voice assistant, and research/data analysis to the relevant documentation pages.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent learning path beginner intermediate advanced getting started roadmap study guide CLI bot Telegram Discord automation voice RL training skills memory cron architecture

pages.title: Nix & NixOS Setup
pages.summary: This page covers installing and running Hermes Agent via Nix flake with three integration levels: nix run / nix profile install for any Nix user, NixOS module (native) for declarative server deployments with systemd and secrets management, and NixOS module (container) for agents needing self-modification via a persistent Ubuntu container. It explains differences from the standard curl installer, prerequisites, quick start, flake configuration, secrets via sops-nix/agenix, MCP server setup, OAuth handling, sampling config, plugin development, and troubleshooting.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent Nix NixOS flake nix run nix profile install uv2nix systemd declarative config sops-nix agenix secrets container mode MCP OAuth plugins direnv nix develop
pages.prerequisites: Nix with flakes enabled; API keys (OpenRouter or Anthropic minimum)

pages.title: Quickstart
pages.summary: This guide gets users from zero to a working Hermes setup. It teaches installing via one-liner, choosing a provider with hermes model, running the first chat, using the TUI, resuming sessions, setting up terminal backends (local, Docker, SSH), installing voice extras, searching and installing skills, adding MCP servers, and using ACP. It emphasizes getting one clean conversation working before layering gateway, cron, skills, voice, or routing.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent quickstart tutorial first setup hermes setup hermes model hermes chat TUI session resume terminal backend Docker SSH voice skills MCP ACP getting started
pages.prerequisites: Linux/macOS/WSL2/Windows WSL2 or Termux; API key for chosen provider

pages.title: Android / Termux
pages.summary: This page documents the tested path for running Hermes Agent on Android phones via Termux. It covers the one-line installer with Termux auto-detection, manual install with pkg and pip, supported features (CLI, cron, PTY, Telegram gateway, MCP, Honcho memory, ACP), and unsupported features (.[all], voice extras due to ctranslate2, Playwright browser bootstrap, Docker isolation). It includes troubleshooting for compilation errors, PATH issues, and Node.js installation.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent Android Termux mobile install one-line installer pkg pip termux extras cron PTY Telegram gateway MCP Honcho ACP ctranslate2 compilation error manual install
code_snippets: pkg install git python clang rust

pages.title: Updating & Uninstalling
pages.summary: This page explains how to update Hermes Agent with hermes update, what happens during an update (pairing-data snapshot, git pull, dependency install, config migration, gateway auto-restart), preview with --check, full backup with --backup, manual update steps, rollback via git checkout, Nix update paths, uninstallation with hermes uninstall or manual cleanup, and stopping the gateway service on Linux/macOS.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent update upgrade uninstall hermes update --check --backup config migration gateway restart git pull uv pip rollback Nix profile uninstall cleanup systemd launchd

pages.title: Integrations
pages.summary: This page is an overview of Hermes Agent integrations with external systems. It covers AI Providers & Routing (OpenRouter, Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, auto-detected capabilities), Provider Routing (sorting, whitelists, blacklists), Fallback Providers, Tool Servers via MCP (stdio and SSE transports, tool filtering), Web Search Backends (Firecrawl, Parallel, Tavily, Exa), Browser Automation (MCP bridge, Playwright, CDP, Browserbase, Camofox), IDE Integration (VS Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Neovim via ACP), Webhooks, and Python SDK.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes Agent integrations AI providers routing fallback MCP servers web search Firecrawl Tavily Exa Parallel browser automation Playwright CDP Browserbase Camofox IDE VS Code Cursor Neovim ACP webhooks Python SDK
pages.prerequisites: Hermes installation; API keys for desired integrations

pages.title: AI Providers
pages.summary: This reference page covers setting up inference providers for Hermes Agent. It documents cloud providers (Nous Portal, OpenAI Codex, GitHub Copilot, Anthropic, OpenRouter, AI Gateway, z.ai/GLM, Kimi/Moonshot, Arcee AI, GMI Cloud, MiniMax, Alibaba Cloud, Kilo Code, StepFun, NVIDIA, Hugging Face), OAuth flows, self-hosted endpoints (Ollama, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp, LM Studio), custom providers (Together, Groq, Perplexity), WSL2 networking setup, provider routing, and fallback configuration. Each provider includes setup commands, env vars, and config.yaml examples.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes AI providers inference OpenRouter Anthropic Copilot OpenAI Nous Portal z.ai GLM Kimi Moonshot MiniMax Alibaba Ollama vLLM SGLang llama.cpp LM Studio custom providers Together Groq Perplexity provider routing fallback WSL2 OAuth API key config
pages.prerequisites: Hermes installation; API key or OAuth for at least one provider

pages.title: CLI Commands Reference
pages.summary: This reference page documents all terminal commands in the Hermes CLI. It covers the global entrypoint hermes [global-options] <command>, global flags (--profile, --resume, --continue, --worktree, --yolo, --tui, --dev), and top-level commands: chat, model, fallback, gateway, setup, whatsapp, slack, auth, status, cron, kanban, webhook, doctor, dump, debug share, backup, checkpoints, import, logs, config, pairing, skills, curator, hooks, memory, acp, mcp, plugins, tools, sessions, insights, claw migrate, dashboard, profile, completion, and update. Each command includes purpose, options, and usage examples.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes CLI commands reference terminal hermes chat model gateway setup auth status cron kanban webhook doctor dump backup logs skills MCP profile update yolo worktree TUI completion

pages.title: Environment Variables
pages.summary: This reference page documents all environment variables used by Hermes Agent, stored in ~/.hermes/.env or set via hermes config set. It covers LLM provider keys (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GLM_API_KEY, KIMI_API_KEY, etc.), base URL overrides, caching (HERMES_OPENROUTER_CACHE, HERMES_OPENROUTER_CACHE_TTL), Copilot tokens, web search backend keys (FIRECRAWL_API_KEY, TAVILY_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, PARALLEL_API_KEY), gateway tokens (TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN), browser/service keys (BROWSERBASE_API_KEY, CAMOFOX_API_KEY), feature toggles (HERMES_TUI, HERMES_VOICE, HERMES_RL), terminal config, compression settings, and fallback provider config.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes environment variables reference .env OPENROUTER_API_KEY OPENAI_API_KEY ANTHROPIC_API_KEY GLM_API_KEY KIMI_API_KEY FIRECRAWL Tavily Exa TELEGRAM DISCORD SLACK BROWSERBASE CAMOFOX HERMES_TUI caching Copilot tokens fallback compression config

pages.title: FAQ & Troubleshooting
pages.summary: This reference page provides quick answers and fixes for common questions and issues. Topics include supported LLM providers, Windows compatibility (WSL2 required), Android/Termux support, local model setup (Ollama, vLLM), programmatic usage via Python SDK, PATH issues, Python version requirements, terminal backend config, provider errors, context length issues, Docker setup for terminal isolation, gateway troubleshooting, WSL2 Chrome control via MCP, compression, MCP setup, subagent delegation, skill/gateway display config, migration from OpenClaw, and backup/restore.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes FAQ troubleshooting Windows WSL2 Android Termux Ollama local model Python SDK PATH Docker gateway MCP context length compression provider error OpenClaw migration backup restore

pages.title: MCP Config Reference
pages.summary: This compact reference page documents the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server configuration schema for Hermes. It covers the root config shape (mcp_servers with command/args/env for stdio or url/headers for HTTP), server keys (enabled, timeout, connect_timeout, auth, sampling), tools policy keys (include, exclude, resources, prompts), filtering semantics, naming convention (mcp_<server>_<tool>), OAuth setup, and utility tool policy. It is the companion to the main MCP conceptual docs.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes MCP config reference Model Context Protocol mcp_servers stdio HTTP command args url headers tools include exclude resources prompts auth OAuth sampling utility tool
code_snippets: stdio server with command and args
HTTP server with url and headers
pages.prerequisites: Hermes installation; basic understanding of MCP

pages.title: Model Catalog
pages.summary: This reference page explains how Hermes fetches curated model lists for OpenRouter and Nous Portal from a JSON manifest hosted on the docs site. It covers the live manifest URL, schema (version, updated_at, metadata, providers with models array), offline fallback to in-repo snapshot, model_catalog config options (enabled, url, ttl_hours, providers), custom provider curation URLs, and the build_model_catalog.py script for regenerating the manifest from hardcoded lists.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes model catalog manifest OpenRouter Nous Portal JSON schema version metadata providers model_catalog config ttl_hours offline fallback build_model_catalog.py curation

pages.title: Optional Skills Catalog
pages.summary: This reference page catalogs optional skills that ship with hermes-agent under optional-skills/ but are not active by default. Users install them with hermes skills install official/<category>/<skill>. Categories include autonomous-ai-agents (blackbox, honcho), blockchain (base, solana), communication (one-three-one-rule), creative (blender-mcp), data (sql, pandas, polars), devops (docker, kubernetes, terraform), finance (yahoo-finance), mlops (flash-attention, onnx-export), security (1password, bitwarden, gpg), and more. Each skill has a dedicated page with setup and usage.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes optional skills catalog blackbox honcho blockchain base solana communication creative blender data sql pandas devops docker kubernetes finance yahoo-finance mlops flash-attention security 1password bitwarden

pages.title: Profile Commands Reference
pages.summary: This reference page documents all commands related to Hermes profiles. It covers hermes profile with subcommands: list, use, create, delete, show, alias, rename, export, import, install, update, and info. Options include --clone, --clone-all, --clone-from, --no-alias, --force, and --yes. It also explains profile distribution format (distribution.yaml), cross-profile command invocation with -p, shell completion setup, and installing profiles from GitHub repos, HTTPS URLs, SSH URLs, or local directories.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes profile commands reference hermes profile list use create delete show alias rename export import install distribution.yaml clone clone-all -p cross-profile

pages.title: Bundled Skills Catalog
pages.summary: This reference page catalogs all built-in skills shipped with Hermes and copied into ~/.hermes/skills/ on install. Skills are organized by category: apple (apple-notes, apple-reminders, findmy, imessage, macos-computer-use), autonomous-ai-agents (claude-code, codex, hermes-agent, opencode), creative (architecture-diagram, ascii-art, ascii-video, baoyu-comic, baoyu-infographic, claude-design, comfyui), data (sql, pandas, polars, yahoo-finance), devops (docker, kubernetes, terraform), and many more. Each entry links to a dedicated page. Skills sync on hermes update but respect local edits.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes bundled skills catalog built-in skills apple-notes claude-code codex architecture-diagram ascii-art comfyui data sql pandas devops docker kubernetes hermes skills reset

pages.title: Slash Commands Reference
pages.summary: This reference page documents Hermes slash commands for both interactive CLI and messaging gateway surfaces, driven by a central COMMAND_REGISTRY. CLI slash commands include session management (/new, /clear, /history, /save, /retry, /undo, /title, /compress), checkpoints (/rollback, /snapshot), process control (/stop, /queue, /steer), model switching (/model), tool management (/tools), gateway (/gateway), skills (/skills), memory (/memory), plans (/plan), and custom quick commands. Messaging slash commands include platform-specific commands and help menus.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes slash commands reference /new /clear /history /compress /rollback /snapshot /stop /queue /steer /model /tools /gateway /skills /memory /plan quick_commands model_aliases CLI messaging

pages.title: Built-in Tools Reference
pages.summary: This reference page documents all 68 built-in tools in the Hermes tool registry, grouped by toolset. It covers browser tools (browser_back, browser_click, browser_console, browser_get_images, browser_navigate, browser_press, browser_scroll, browser_snapshot, browser_type, browser_vision, web_search), file tools (read_file, write_file, patch, search_files), terminal tools (execute_command, shell), web tools (web_search, web_extract), RL tools, Home Assistant tools, Feishu tools, Spotify tools, Yuanbao tools, Discord tools, and MCP tools (server-name prefixed). Availability varies by platform, credentials, and enabled toolsets.

pages.title: Toolsets Reference
pages.summary: This reference page documents toolsets, which are named bundles of tools controlling what the agent can do. It explains three kinds of toolsets: Core (single logical group like file, browser, terminal), Composite (combines multiple core toolsets like debugging), and Platform (complete configuration for a deployment context like hermes-cli). It covers per-session CLI configuration (--toolsets), per-platform config.yaml configuration, interactive management via hermes tools and in-session /tools commands, and a table of all core toolsets with their included tools.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes toolsets reference core composite platform browser file terminal web rl debugging hermes-cli custom_toolsets hermes chat --toolsets config.yaml

Each skill links to its dedicated documentation page. Platform compatibility is shown with Linux, macOS, and Windows icons.

pages.title: Nix & NixOS Setup

pages.title: Configuring Models
pages.summary: Hermes uses two kinds of model slots: Main model — what the agent thinks with. Every user message, every tool-call loop, every streamed response goes through this model. Auxiliary models — smaller side-jobs the agent offloads. Context compression, vision (image analysis), web-page summarization, session search, approval scoring, MCP tool routing, session-title generation, and skill search. Each has its own slot and can be overridden independently. This page covers configuring both from the dashboard. If you prefer config files or the CLI, jump to Alternative methods at the bottom.

pages.title: Docker
pages.summary: There are two distinct ways Docker intersects with Hermes Agent: Running Hermes IN Docker — the agent itself runs inside a container (this page's primary focus) Docker as a terminal backend — the agent runs on your host but executes every command inside a single, persistent Docker sandbox container that survives across tool calls, /new , and subagents for the life of the Hermes process (see Configuration → Docker Backend ) This page covers option 1. The container stores all user data (config, API keys, sessions, skills, memories) in a single directory mounted from the host at /opt/data .

pages.title: API Server
pages.summary: The API server exposes hermes-agent as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP endpoint. Any frontend that speaks the OpenAI format — Open WebUI, LobeChat, LibreChat, NextChat, ChatBox, and hundreds more — can connect to hermes-agent and use it as a backend. Your agent handles requests with its full toolset (terminal, file operations, web search, memory, skills) and returns the final response. When streaming, tool progress indicators appear inline so frontends can show what the agent is doing.

pages.title: Batch Processing
pages.summary: Batch processing lets you run the Hermes agent across hundreds or thousands of prompts in parallel, generating structured trajectory data. This is primarily used for training data generation — producing ShareGPT-format trajectories with tool usage statistics that can be used for fine-tuning or evaluation. Overview ​ The batch runner ( batch_runner.py ) processes a JSONL dataset of prompts, running each through a full agent session with tool access. Each prompt gets its own isolated environment.

pages.title: Computer Use (macOS)
pages.summary: Hermes Agent can drive your Mac's desktop — clicking, typing, scrolling, dragging — in the background . Your cursor doesn't move, keyboard focus doesn't change, and macOS doesn't switch Spaces on you. You and the agent co-work on the same machine. Unlike most computer-use integrations, this works with any tool-capable model — Claude, GPT, Gemini, or an open model on a local vLLM endpoint. There's no Anthropic-native schema to worry about.

code_snippets: HERMES_COMPUTER_USE_BACKEND=noop   # records calls, no side effects

pages.title: Credential Pools
pages.summary: Credential pools let you register multiple API keys or OAuth tokens for the same provider. When one key hits a rate limit or billing quota, Hermes automatically rotates to the next healthy key — keeping your session alive without switching providers. This is different from fallback providers , which switch to a different provider entirely. Credential pools are same-provider rotation; fallback providers are cross-provider failover. Pools are tried first — if all pool keys are exhausted, then the fallback provider activates.
pages.embedding_keywords: credential pools credential-pools auth json api key type config yaml hermes exhaustion pool flow runtime provider oauth anthropic openrouter keys custom rotation second via next endpoint together

pages.title: Curator
pages.summary: The curator is a background maintenance pass for agent-created skills . It tracks how often each skill is viewed, used, and patched, moves long-unused skills through active → stale → archived states, and periodically spawns a short auxiliary-model review that proposes consolidations or patches drift. It exists so that skills created via the self-improvement loop don't pile up forever. Every time the agent solves a novel problem and saves a skill, that skill lands in ~/.hermes/skills/ .
pages.embedding_keywords: curator hermes skills config yaml dry list archive logs id reason usage json skill agent model pin review rollback active auxiliary llm snapshot name

Configuration ​ The easiest path is the interactive manager: hermes fallback hermes fallback reuses the provider picker from hermes model — same provider list, same credential prompts, same validation.

pages.title: Persistent Goals
pages.summary: /goal gives Hermes a standing objective that survives across turns. After every turn a lightweight judge model checks whether the goal is satisfied by the assistant's last response. If not, Hermes automatically feeds a continuation prompt back into the same session and keeps working — until the goal is achieved, you pause or clear it, or the turn budget runs out. It's our take on the Ralph loop , directly inspired by Codex CLI 0.128.0's /goal by Eric Traut (OpenAI). The core idea — keep a goal alive across turns and don't stop until it's achieved — is theirs.
pages.embedding_keywords: persistent goals goal tmp note resume config yaml hermes gemini pause clear judge turn turns txt files model continuation message text budget toward prompt

pages.title: Honcho Memory
pages.summary: Honcho is an AI-native memory backend that adds dialectic reasoning and deep user modeling on top of Hermes's built-in memory system. Instead of simple key-value storage, Honcho maintains a running model of who the user is — their preferences, communication style, goals, and patterns — by reasoning about conversations after they happen. Honcho is a Memory Provider Plugin Honcho is integrated into the Memory Providers system. All features below are available through the unified memory provider interface.

pages.title: Kanban (Multi-Agent Board)
pages.summary: Read the Kanban tutorial — four user stories (solo dev, fleet farming, role pipeline with retry, circuit breaker) with dashboard screenshots of each. This page is the reference; the tutorial is the narrative. Hermes Kanban is a durable task board, shared across all your Hermes profiles, that lets multiple named agents collaborate on work without fragile in-process subagent swarms. Every task is a row in ~/.hermes/kanban.db ; every handoff is a row anyone can read and write; every worker is a full OS process with its own identity.

pages.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
pages.summary: MCP lets Hermes Agent connect to external tool servers so the agent can use tools that live outside Hermes itself — GitHub, databases, file systems, browser stacks, internal APIs, and more. If you have ever wanted Hermes to use a tool that already exists somewhere else, MCP is usually the cleanest way to do it.

pages.title: Persistent Memory
pages.summary: Hermes Agent has bounded, curated memory that persists across sessions. This lets it remember your preferences, your projects, your environment, and things it has learned. How It Works ​ Two files make up the agent's memory: File Purpose Char Limit MEMORY.md Agent's personal notes — environment facts, conventions, things learned 2,200 chars (~800 tokens) USER.md User profile — your preferences, communication style, expectations 1,375 chars (~500 tokens) Both are stored in ~/.hermes/memories/ and are injected into the system prompt as a frozen snapshot at session start.
pages.embedding_keywords: persistent memory memories myapi removes obvious staging state postgresql hermes db config yaml user entries agent session system search prompt project entry save facts chars tool

# Good: Packs multiple related factsUser runs macOS 14 Sonoma, uses Homebrew, has Docker Desktop and Podman. Shell: z...
hermes sessions list    # Browse past sessions

pages.title: Memory Providers
pages.summary: Hermes Agent ships with 8 external memory provider plugins that give the agent persistent, cross-session knowledge beyond the built-in MEMORY.md and USER.md. Only one external provider can be active at a time — the built-in memory is always active alongside it. Quick Start ​ hermes memory setup # interactive picker + configuration hermes memory status # check what's active hermes memory off # disable external provider You can also select the active memory provider via hermes plugins → Provider Plugins → Memory Provider.
pages.embedding_keywords: memory providers memory-providers hermes env setup byterover honcho best for openviking clone config yaml tools profile user context provider key true peer cloud search session per hindsight supermemory local api
code_snippets: hermes memory setup      # interactive picker + configuration hermes memory status     # check what's active hermes m...
memory:  provider: openviking   # or honcho, mem0, hindsight, holographic, retaindb, byterover, supermemory
hermes honcho setup        # (legacy command) # or hermes memory setup        # select "honcho"

pages.title: Features Overview
pages.summary: Hermes Agent includes a rich set of capabilities that extend far beyond basic chat. From persistent memory and file-aware context to browser automation and voice conversations, these features work together to make Hermes a powerful autonomous assistant. Core ​ Tools & Toolsets — Tools are functions that extend the agent's capabilities. They're organized into logical toolsets that can be enabled or disabled per platform, covering web search, terminal execution, file editing, memory, delegation, and more. Skills System — On-demand knowledge documents the agent can load when needed.
pages.embedding_keywords: features overview soul md resume sharegpt elevenlabs minimax neutts kittentts github openviking byterover hermes agent memory tools context voice file via tool providers across code hooks browser automation

pages.title: Personality & SOUL.md
pages.summary: Hermes Agent's personality is fully customizable. SOUL.md is the primary identity — it's the first thing in the system prompt and defines who the agent is. How SOUL.md works now ​ Hermes now seeds a default SOUL.md automatically in: ~/.hermes/SOUL.md More precisely, it uses the current instance's HERMES_HOME , so if you run Hermes with a custom home directory, it will use: $HERMES_HOME/SOUL.md Important behavior ​ SOUL.md is the agent's primary identity. It occupies slot #1 in the system prompt, replacing the hardcoded default identity.

pages.title: Provider Routing

OpenRouter routes requests to many providers (e.g., Anthropic, Google, AWS Bedrock, Together AI). Provider routing lets you optimize for cost, speed, quality, or enforce specific provider requirements.

pages.title: RL Training
pages.summary: Hermes Agent includes an integrated RL (Reinforcement Learning) training pipeline built on Tinker-Atropos . This enables training language models on environment-specific tasks using GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization) with LoRA adapters, orchestrated entirely through the agent's tool interface.

pages.title: Skills System
pages.summary: Skills are on-demand knowledge documents the agent can load when needed. They follow a progressive disclosure pattern to minimize token usage and are compatible with the agentskills.io open standard. All skills live in ~/.hermes/skills/ — the primary directory and source of truth. On fresh install, bundled skills are copied from the repo. Hub-installed and agent-created skills also go here. The agent can modify or delete any skill. You can also point Hermes at external skill directories — additional folders scanned alongside the local one. See External Skill Directories below.

pages.title: Spotify
pages.summary: Hermes can control Spotify directly — playback, queue, search, playlists, saved tracks/albums, and listening history — using Spotify's official Web API with PKCE OAuth. Tokens are stored in ~/.hermes/auth.json and refreshed automatically on 401; you only log in once per machine. Unlike Hermes' built-in OAuth integrations (Google, GitHub Copilot, Codex), Spotify requires every user to register their own lightweight developer app. Spotify does not let third parties ship a public OAuth app that anyone can use. It takes about two minutes and hermes auth spotify walks you through it.

pages.title: Git Worktrees
pages.summary: Hermes Agent is often used on large, long‑lived repositories. When you want to: Run multiple agents in parallel on the same project, or Keep experimental refactors isolated from your main branch, Git worktrees are the safest way to give each agent its own checkout without duplicating the entire repository. This page shows how to combine worktrees with Hermes so each session has a clean, isolated working directory. Why Use Worktrees with Hermes?

cd ../repo-feature # Start Hermes in the worktree hermes
cd /path/to/your/repo git worktree add ../repo-experiment-a feature/hermes-a git worktree add ../repo-experiment-b fe...
# Terminal 1 cd ../repo-experiment-a hermes # Terminal 2 cd ../repo-experiment-b hermes
cd /path/to/your/repo # Remove the worktree directory and its reference git worktree remove ../repo-feature

pages.title: DingTalk
pages.summary: Hermes Agent integrates with DingTalk (钉钉) as a chatbot, letting you chat with your AI assistant through direct messages or group chats. The bot connects via DingTalk's Stream Mode — a long-lived WebSocket connection that requires no public URL or webhook server — and replies using markdown-formatted messages through DingTalk's session webhook API. Before setup, here's the part most people want to know: how Hermes behaves once it's in your DingTalk workspace. How Hermes Behaves ​ Context Behavior DMs (1:1 chat) Hermes responds to every message. Each DM has its own session.

pages.title: Email
pages.summary: Hermes can receive and reply to emails using standard IMAP and SMTP protocols. Send an email to the agent's address and it replies in-thread — no special client or bot API needed. Works with Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo, Fastmail, or any provider that supports IMAP/SMTP. No External Dependencies The Email adapter uses Python's built-in imaplib , smtplib , and email modules. No additional packages or external services are required.

pages.title: Matrix
pages.summary: Hermes Agent integrates with Matrix, the open, federated messaging protocol. Matrix lets you run your own homeserver or use a public one like matrix.org — either way, you keep control of your communications. The bot connects via the mautrix Python SDK, processes messages through the Hermes Agent pipeline (including tool use, memory, and reasoning), and responds in real time. It supports text, file attachments, images, audio, video, and optional end-to-end encryption (E2EE). Hermes works with any Matrix homeserver — Synapse, Conduit, Dendrite, or matrix.org.

pages.title: Microsoft Graph Webhook Listener
pages.summary: The msgraph_webhook gateway platform is an inbound event listener. It's how Hermes receives change notifications from Microsoft Graph — "a Teams meeting ended," "a new message landed in this chat," "this calendar event was updated." Different from the teams platform (which is a chat bot users type to) — this one is M365 telling Hermes something happened, not a person. Right now the primary consumer is the Teams meeting summary pipeline: Graph notifies when a meeting produces a transcript, the pipeline fetches it, and Hermes posts a summary back into Teams.

pages.title: Signal
pages.summary: Hermes connects to Signal through the signal-cli daemon running in HTTP mode. The adapter streams messages in real-time via SSE (Server-Sent Events) and sends responses via JSON-RPC. Signal is the most privacy-focused mainstream messenger — end-to-end encrypted by default, open-source protocol, minimal metadata collection. This makes it ideal for security-sensitive agent workflows. No New Python Dependencies The Signal adapter uses httpx (already a core Hermes dependency) for all communication. No additional Python packages are required. You just need signal-cli installed externally.

pages.title: Microsoft Teams
pages.summary: Connect Hermes Agent to Microsoft Teams as a bot. Unlike Slack's Socket Mode, Teams delivers messages by calling a public HTTPS webhook , so your instance needs a publicly reachable endpoint — either a dev tunnel (local dev) or a real domain (production). Need meeting summaries from Microsoft Graph events rather than normal bot conversations? Use the dedicated setup page: Teams Meetings . How the Bot Responds ​ Context Behavior Personal chat (DM) Bot responds to every message. Group chat Bot only responds when @mentioned. Channel Bot only responds when @mentioned.

pages.title: Telegram
pages.summary: Hermes Agent integrates with Telegram as a full-featured conversational bot. Once connected, you can chat with your agent from any device, send voice memos that get auto-transcribed, receive scheduled task results, and use the agent in group chats. The integration is built on python-telegram-bot and supports text, voice, images, and file attachments. Step 1: Create a Bot via BotFather ​ Every Telegram bot requires an API token issued by @BotFather , Telegram's official bot management tool.

code_snippets: help - Show help informationnew - Start a new conversationsethome - Set this chat as the home channel

pages.title: Webhooks
pages.summary: Receive events from external services (GitHub, GitLab, JIRA, Stripe, etc.) and trigger Hermes agent runs automatically. The webhook adapter runs an HTTP server that accepts POST requests, validates HMAC signatures, transforms payloads into agent prompts, and routes responses back to the source or to another configured platform. The agent processes the event and can respond by posting comments on PRs, sending messages to Telegram/Discord, or logging the result.

pages.title: WeCom (Enterprise WeChat)
pages.summary: Connect Hermes to WeCom (企业微信), Tencent's enterprise messaging platform. The adapter uses WeCom's AI Bot WebSocket gateway for real-time bidirectional communication — no public endpoint or webhook needed. Prerequisites ​ A WeCom organization account An AI Bot created in the WeCom Admin Console The Bot ID and Secret from the bot's credentials page Python packages: aiohttp and httpx Setup ​ Step 1: Create an AI Bot ​ Recommended: Scan-to-Create (one command) ​ hermes gateway setup Select WeCom and scan the QR code with your WeCom mobile app.

pages.title: WeCom Callback (Self-Built App)

WeCom Bot vs WeCom Callback Hermes supports two WeCom integration modes: WeCom Bot — bot-style, connects via WebSocket. Simpler setup, works in group chats. WeCom Callback (this page) — self-built app, receives encrypted XML callbacks. Shows as a first-class app in users' WeCom sidebar. Supports multi-corp routing.

pages.title: Weixin (WeChat)
pages.summary: Connect Hermes to WeChat (微信), Tencent's personal messaging platform. The adapter uses Tencent's iLink Bot API for personal WeChat accounts — this is distinct from WeCom (Enterprise WeChat). Messages are delivered via long-polling, so no public endpoint or webhook is required. This adapter is for personal WeChat accounts (微信). If you need enterprise/corporate WeChat, see the WeCom adapter instead. iLink bot identity — ordinary WeChat groups may not work QR login connects Hermes to an iLink bot identity (e.g. a5ace6fd482e@im.bot ), not a fully scriptable ordinary personal WeChat account.

pages.title: Yuanbao
pages.summary: Connect Hermes to Yuanbao , Tencent's enterprise messaging platform. The adapter uses a WebSocket gateway for real-time message delivery and supports both direct (C2C) and group conversations. Yuanbao is an enterprise messaging platform primarily used within Tencent and enterprise environments. It uses WebSocket for real-time communication, HMAC-based authentication, and supports rich media including images, files, and voice messages.

pages.title: Profile Distributions: Share a Whole Agent
pages.summary: A profile distribution packages a complete Hermes agent — personality, skills, cron jobs, MCP connections, config — as a git repository. Anyone with access to the repo can install the whole agent with one command, update it in place, and keep their own memories, sessions, and API keys untouched. If a profile is a local agent, a distribution is that agent made shareable.

pages.title: Profiles: Running Multiple Agents
pages.summary: Run multiple independent Hermes agents on the same machine — each with its own config, API keys, memory, sessions, skills, and gateway state. ​ A profile is a separate Hermes home directory. Each profile gets its own directory containing its own config.yaml , .env , SOUL.md , memories, sessions, skills, cron jobs, and state database. Profiles let you run separate agents for different purposes — a coding assistant, a personal bot, a research agent — without mixing up Hermes state. When you create a profile, it automatically becomes its own command.

pages.title: Sessions
pages.summary: Hermes Agent automatically saves every conversation as a session. Sessions enable conversation resume, cross-session search, and full conversation history management. How Sessions Work ​ Every conversation — whether from the CLI, Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Matrix, Teams, or any other messaging platform — is stored as a session with full message history.

pages.title: Windows (Native) Guide — Early Beta
pages.summary: Early BETA Native Windows support is early beta . It installs, runs, and passes our Windows-footgun lint, but it hasn't been road-tested at the scale our Linux/macOS/WSL2 paths have. Expect rough edges — especially around subprocess handling, path quirks, and non-ASCII console output. Please file issues with repro steps when you hit something. If you want a battle-tested setup today, use the Linux/macOS installer under WSL2 instead. Hermes runs natively on Windows 10 and Windows 11 — no WSL, no Cygwin, no Docker.

pages.title: Windows (WSL2) Guide
pages.summary: Hermes Agent now supports both native Windows and WSL2. This page covers the WSL2 path; for the native PowerShell install see the dedicated Windows (Native) Guide . When to pick WSL2 over native: You want to use the dashboard's embedded terminal ( /chat tab) — that pane requires a POSIX PTY and is WSL2-only. You're doing POSIX-heavy development work and want your Hermes sessions to share the same filesystem / paths as your dev tools. You already have a WSL2 environment and don't want to maintain a second install.

pages.title: Credential Pools
pages.summary: Credential pools let you register multiple API keys or OAuth tokens for the same provider. When one key hits a rate limit or billing quota, Hermes automatically rotates to the next healthy key — keeping your session alive without switching providers. This is different from fallback providers , which switch to a different provider entirely.

pages.title: Honcho Memory
pages.summary: Honcho is an AI-native memory backend that adds dialectic reasoning and deep user modeling on top of Hermes's built-in memory system. Instead of simple key-value storage, Honcho maintains a running model of who the user is — their preferences, communication style, goals, and patterns — by reasoning about conversations after they happen.

Use instead of execute_code when building state incrementally, exploring APIs, inspecting DataFrames, or iterating on complex code. Requires uv and JupyterLab installed. Clone the hamelnb repo to ~/.agent-skills/hamelnb and start the Jupyter server. The script connects to the running kernel for iterative exploration.
pages.embedding_keywords: Jupyter live kernel hamelnb REPL stateful Python data science DataFrame iterative exploration uv JupyterLab
pages.prerequisites: uv installed; JupyterLab installed via uv; clone hamelnb repo

pages.title: Kanban Orchestrator
pages.summary: This skill provides a decomposition playbook, specialist-roster conventions, and anti-temptation rules for an orchestrator profile routing work through Kanban. The 'don't do the work yourself' rule and basic lifecycle are auto-injected into every kanban worker's system prompt. Create Kanban tasks when multiple specialists are needed, work should survive crashes, human-in-the-loop is expected, subtasks can run in parallel, review/iteration is expected, or audit trail matters.
pages.embedding_keywords: Kanban orchestrator multi-agent decomposition routing specialist roster audit trail fan-out workflow

pages.title: Kanban Worker — Pitfalls, examples, and edge cases for Hermes Kanban workers
pages.summary: This skill covers pitfalls, examples, and edge cases for Hermes Kanban workers. The lifecycle (6 steps: orient, work, heartbeat, block/complete) is auto-injected via KANBAN_GUIDANCE. This skill provides deeper detail on workspace handling (scratch, dir, worktree), good handoff shapes, retry diagnostics, and edge cases. Workspace kind determines behavior: scratch dirs are temporary and GC'd, persistent dirs are shared state, and git worktrees require branch awareness.
pages.embedding_keywords: Kanban worker lifecycle workspace scratch worktree handoff retry diagnostics edge cases multi-agent
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Kanban system enabled; dispatched as kanban worker

pages.title: Webhook Subscriptions — Webhook subscriptions: event-driven agent runs
pages.summary: This skill creates dynamic webhook subscriptions so external services (GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, CI/CD, IoT sensors, monitoring tools) can trigger Hermes agent runs by POSTing events. Setup options include a wizard (hermes gateway setup), manual config in ~/.hermes/config.yaml, or environment variables. After configuration, start/restart the gateway. Supports creating, listing, deleting, and testing subscriptions with HMAC signature verification.
pages.embedding_keywords: webhook event-driven automation GitHub GitLab Stripe CI/CD IoT HMAC gateway subscription Hermes
pages.prerequisites: Hermes gateway installed; configure webhook platform in config.yaml or env vars; set HMAC secret

pages.title: Dogfood — Exploratory QA of web apps: find bugs, evidence, reports
pages.summary: This skill guides systematic exploratory QA testing of web applications using the browser toolset. Follows a 5-phase workflow: Plan (create output directory, identify scope, build sitemap), Navigate (visit pages, interact with elements), Test (functional, boundary, error, compatibility, performance tests), Capture (screenshots, console logs, network errors), and Report (structured bug report with severity, reproduction steps, evidence). Requires browser toolset availability.
pages.embedding_keywords: QA testing exploratory bug report web app browser screenshot evidence dogfood automation
pages.prerequisites: Browser toolset available (browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click, etc.)

pages.title: Himalaya — Himalaya CLI: IMAP/SMTP email from terminal
pages.summary: This skill manages emails via the Himalaya CLI using IMAP, SMTP, Notmuch, or Sendmail backends. Supports reading, sending, searching, and managing emails from the terminal. Installation via pre-built binary (Linux/macOS), Homebrew, or cargo. Configuration via interactive wizard (himalaya account configure) or manual ~/.config/himalaya/config.toml. References include configuration.md for auth setup and message-composition.md for MML syntax.
pages.embedding_keywords: Himalaya CLI email IMAP SMTP terminal Notmuch Sendmail MML configuration wizard Linux macOS
pages.prerequisites: Himalaya CLI installed; IMAP/SMTP credentials; config file at ~/.config/himalaya/config.toml

pages.title: Minecraft Modpack Server — Host modded Minecraft servers (CurseForge, Modrinth)
pages.summary: This skill sets up modded Minecraft servers from server pack zips using NeoForge/Forge. Gathers user preferences first: server name/MOTD, seed, difficulty, gamemode, online mode, player count, RAM allocation, view distance, PvP, whitelist, and backups. Steps include downloading and inspecting the pack, setting up the server directory, configuring server.properties, installing mods, and tuning performance. Provides sensible defaults but asks before generating config.
pages.embedding_keywords: Minecraft modpack server NeoForge Forge CurseForge Modrinth server.properties gaming hosting multiplayer
pages.prerequisites: Java; server pack zip file; sufficient RAM

pages.title: Pokemon Player — Play Pokemon via headless emulator + RAM reads
pages.summary: This skill plays Pokemon games via headless emulation using the pokemon-agent package. Supports Pokemon Red, Blue, Yellow, FireRed, etc. Startup procedure: clone NousResearch/pokemon-agent repo, set up Python 3.10+ venv with uv, install package with pyboy extra, and ask user for ROM file (NEVER download ROMs). Start pokemon-agent serve with --rom and --port 9876 in background. Can resume from saved game with --load-state. Wait 4 seconds after startup.
pages.embedding_keywords: Pokemon headless emulator pokemon-agent pyboy ROM Game Boy GBA gaming AI playthrough save state
pages.prerequisites: Python 3.10+; uv or pip; user-provided ROM file; clone pokemon-agent repo

pages.title: Codebase Inspection — Inspect codebases w/ pygount: LOC, languages, ratios
pages.summary: This skill analyzes repositories for lines of code, language breakdown, file counts, and code-vs-comment ratios using pygount. Use when users ask for LOC counts, language breakdowns, codebase size, or composition. Always use --folders-to-skip to exclude dependency/build directories (.git, node_modules, venv, __pycache__, dist, build, .next, .tox). Supports summary and per-file output formats. Folder exclusions should be adjusted based on project type.
pages.embedding_keywords: pygount LOC lines of code language breakdown repository analysis metrics comment ratio file count codebase

pages.title: Github Auth — GitHub auth setup: HTTPS tokens, SSH keys, gh CLI login
pages.summary: This skill sets up GitHub authentication for agent use with repositories, PRs, issues, and CI. Two paths: git (HTTPS tokens or SSH keys, always available) and gh CLI (richer API access, simpler auth). Detection flow checks git --version, gh --version, gh auth status, and git credential helper. If gh is authenticated, use it for everything. If gh is installed but not auth'd, use gh auth method. If no gh, use git-only method with no sudo needed.
pages.embedding_keywords: GitHub auth authentication gh CLI SSH key HTTPS token personal access token credential helper login setup
pages.prerequisites: Git installed; optionally gh CLI; GitHub account

pages.title: Github Code Review — Review PRs: diffs, inline comments via gh or REST
pages.summary: This skill performs code reviews on local changes before pushing, or reviews open PRs on GitHub. Most operations use plain git; gh/curl only matter for PR-level interactions. Auth detection checks for gh CLI or GITHUB_TOKEN from ~/.hermes/.env or ~/.git-credentials. Supports reviewing diffs, adding inline comments, and managing review states. Related to github-auth for authentication setup.
pages.embedding_keywords: GitHub code review PR diff inline comments gh CLI REST API GITHUB_TOKEN git review approval
pages.prerequisites: GitHub authenticated (see github-auth skill); inside a git repository

pages.title: Github Issues — Create, triage, label, assign GitHub issues via gh or REST
pages.summary: This skill creates, searches, triages, and manages GitHub issues via gh CLI or curl fallback. Auth detection mirrors other GitHub skills (gh CLI preferred, then GITHUB_TOKEN from env or credentials). Supports creating issues with labels/assignees, searching by state/label/assignee, listing repository issues, updating and closing issues. Each section shows gh commands first, then curl alternatives.
pages.embedding_keywords: GitHub issues triage labels assignees bug tracking gh CLI create search close project management

pages.title: Github Pr Workflow — GitHub PR lifecycle: branch, commit, open, CI, merge
pages.summary: This skill manages the complete GitHub Pull Request lifecycle: creating branches, committing changes, opening PRs, handling CI checks, and merging. Each section shows gh CLI commands first, then git+curl fallbacks for machines without gh. Covers branch creation, pushing, PR creation with body templates, draft PRs, checking CI status, requesting reviews, and merge strategies (merge, squash, rebase). Auth detection mirrors other GitHub skills.
pages.embedding_keywords: GitHub PR workflow branch commit open CI merge squash rebase gh CLI draft review lifecycle

pages.title: Github Repo Management — Clone/create/fork repos; manage remotes, releases
pages.summary: This skill creates, clones, forks, configures, and manages GitHub repositories. Covers repo creation (private/public), cloning, forking, managing remotes, setting up branch protection, managing secrets/variables, and creating releases. Each section shows gh CLI first, then git+curl fallbacks. Auth detection follows the same pattern as other GitHub skills. Also covers listing user repos and transferring ownership.
pages.embedding_keywords: GitHub repository clone fork create remote release branch protection secrets variables management

pages.title: Native Mcp — MCP client: connect servers, register tools (stdio/HTTP)
pages.summary: This skill uses Hermes Agent's built-in MCP client to connect to MCP servers at startup, discover their tools, and make them available as first-class tools. Supports local stdio-based servers (npx, uvx) and remote HTTP/StreamableHTTP MCP servers. No bridge CLI needed — tools appear alongside built-in tools. Requires mcp Python package (pip install mcp), Node.js for npx-based servers, and uv for uvx-based servers. For ad-hoc one-off calls, see mcporter skill instead.
pages.embedding_keywords: MCP Model Context Protocol native client stdio HTTP StreamableHTTP npx uvx tool discovery server connection

pages.title: Gif Search — Search/download GIFs from Tenor via curl + jq
pages.summary: This skill searches and downloads GIFs via the Tenor API using curl and jq. No extra tools needed beyond standard macOS/Linux utilities. Setup requires a free Tenor API key from Google Cloud Console (stored as TENOR_API_KEY in ~/.hermes/.env). Supports searching with query strings, getting different formats (gif, tinygif), and downloading top results. Useful for reaction GIFs and visual content creation.
pages.embedding_keywords: GIF search Tenor API curl jq download reaction Google Cloud API key media creative

pages.title: Heartmula — HeartMuLa: Suno-like song generation from lyrics + tags
pages.summary: This skill generates music using HeartMuLa, an open-source music foundation model family (Apache-2.0) comparable to Suno. Generates full songs from lyrics and tags with multilingual support. Components include HeartMuLa (3B/7B music language model), HeartCodec (12.5Hz music codec), HeartTranscriptor (Whisper-based lyrics transcription), and HeartCLAP (audio-text alignment). Minimum 8GB VRAM with --lazy_load true; recommended 16GB+. Supports multi-GPU splitting.
pages.embedding_keywords: HeartMuLa music generation Suno alternative open source lyrics tags multilingual HeartCodec GPU VRAM Apache
pages.prerequisites: Python; GPU with 8GB+ VRAM (16GB+ recommended); clone HeartMuLa repo; pip install

pages.title: Songsee — Audio spectrograms/features (mel, chroma, MFCC) via CLI
pages.summary: This skill generates spectrograms and multi-panel audio feature visualizations from audio files via the songsee CLI. Written in Go. Supports standard spectrogram, mel-scaled spectrogram, chroma (pitch class), HPSS (harmonic/percussive separation), self-similarity matrix, loudness, tempogram, MFCC, and spectral flux. Multiple --viz types render as a grid in a single image. Optional ffmpeg for formats beyond WAV/MP3. Time slicing supported.
pages.embedding_keywords: songsee spectrogram mel chroma MFCC audio visualization Go CLI HPSS spectral flux tempogram loudness
pages.prerequisites: Go installed; optional ffmpeg for non-WAV/MP3 formats

pages.title: Spotify — Spotify: play, search, queue, manage playlists and devices
pages.summary: This skill controls the user's Spotify account via the Hermes Spotify toolset (7 tools). Playback-mutating actions require Spotify Premium; search/library/playlist ops work on Free. Tools include spotify_playback (play, pause, next, seek, volume), spotify_devices (list, transfer), spotify_queue (get, add), spotify_search, spotify_playlists (create, add, remove), spotify_albums, and spotify_library. Setup guide referenced at /docs/user-guide/features/spotify.
pages.embedding_keywords: Spotify playback search queue playlist device control Premium music media Hermes tools
pages.prerequisites: Spotify account; Premium for playback controls; Hermes Spotify tools configured

pages.title: Youtube Content — YouTube transcripts to summaries, threads, blogs
pages.summary: This skill extracts transcripts from YouTube videos and converts them into useful formats. Install youtube-transcript-api via pip. The helper script fetch_transcript.py accepts any standard YouTube URL format, short links, shorts, embeds, live links, or raw video ID. Output formats include JSON with metadata, plain text, text with timestamps, and specific language with fallback chain. After fetching, format based on user request: chapters, summary, threads, or blog posts.
pages.embedding_keywords: YouTube transcript video summary chapters blog thread timestamps youtube-transcript-api content extraction

pages.title: Evaluating Llms Harness — lm-eval-harness: benchmark LLMs (MMLU, GSM8K, etc)
pages.summary: This skill evaluates LLMs across 60+ academic benchmarks (MMLU, HumanEval, GSM8K, TruthfulQA, HellaSwag) using lm-evaluation-harness. Industry standard used by EleutherAI, HuggingFace, and major labs. Supports HuggingFace models, vLLM, and APIs. Installation via pip install lm-eval. Quick start shows evaluating any HF model with task selection and device specification. Common workflows include standard benchmark evaluation and viewing available tasks.
pages.embedding_keywords: lm-eval-harness LLM benchmark MMLU GSM8K HumanEval TruthfulQA EleutherAI HuggingFace evaluation metrics
pages.prerequisites: Python; pip install lm-eval; GPU recommended for large models

pages.summary: This skill uses Weights & Biases (W&B) for ML experiment tracking and MLOps. Features include tracking experiments with automatic metric logging, real-time training dashboards, comparing runs across hyperparameters, automated hyperparameter sweeps, model registry with versioning and lineage, team workspaces, and artifact tracking (datasets, models, code). 200,000+ practitioners, 10.5k+ GitHub stars, 100+ integrations. Install via pip install wandb and login for API key.
pages.embedding_keywords: Weights Biases W&B experiment tracking MLOps hyperparameter sweep model registry dashboard artifact PyTorch TensorFlow

pages.title: Huggingface Hub — HuggingFace hf CLI: search/download/upload models, datasets
pages.summary: This skill uses the modern hf CLI for interacting with the Hugging Face Hub, replacing the deprecated huggingface-cli. Supports managing repositories, models, datasets, and Spaces. Core commands include hf download, hf upload, hf upload-large-folder (resumable), hf sync, and hf env. Authentication via HF_TOKEN env var or --token flag. hf auth login/logout manage sessions. The CLI also supports repo creation, file management, and dataset operations.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hugging Face Hub hf CLI model dataset download upload authentication HF_TOKEN Spaces repository

pages.title: Llama Cpp — llama.cpp local GGUF inference + HF Hub model discovery
pages.summary: This skill handles local GGUF inference, quantization selection, and Hugging Face repo discovery for llama.cpp. Use for running local models on CPU, Apple Silicon, CUDA, ROCm, or Intel GPUs. Model discovery prefers URL workflows: search Hub with apps=llama.cpp filter, open repos with local-app=llama.cpp view, and copy exact llama-server or llama-cli snippets. Supports Q4/Q5/Q6/IQ variant selection based on RAM/VRAM. Dependencies: llama-cpp-python>=0.2.0.
pages.embedding_keywords: llama.cpp GGUF quantization local inference CPU Apple Silicon CUDA ROCm Hugging Face model discovery

pages.title: Obliteratus — OBLITERATUS: abliterate LLM refusals (diff-in-means)
pages.summary: This skill removes refusal behaviors (guardrails) from open-weight LLMs without retraining or fine-tuning. Uses mechanistic interpretability techniques including diff-in-means, SVD, whitened SVD, LEACE concept erasure, SAE decomposition, and Bayesian kernel projection. Includes 9 CLI methods, 28 analysis modules, 116 model presets across 5 compute tiers, tournament evaluation, and telemetry-driven recommendations. License warning: AGPL-3.0 — always invoke via CLI, never import as a Python library.
pages.embedding_keywords: OBLITERATUS abliteration refusal removal mechanistic interpretability diff-in-means SVD LEACE SAE open-weight LLM
pages.prerequisites: Python; torch, transformers, bitsandbytes, accelerate, safetensors; GPU recommended; invoke via CLI only

pages.title: Outlines — Outlines: structured JSON/regex/Pydantic LLM generation
pages.summary: This skill uses Outlines for structured text generation with LLMs. Guarantees valid JSON/XML/code structure, supports Pydantic models for type-safe outputs, works with local models (Transformers, llama.cpp, vLLM), maximizes inference speed with zero-overhead structured generation, and controls token sampling at the grammar level. 8,000+ GitHub stars from dottxt.ai. Install base with pip install outlines, or with specific backends: transformers, llama-cpp-python, or vLLM.
pages.embedding_keywords: Outlines structured generation JSON Pydantic regex grammar local model vLLM transformers type safety

pages.title: Serving Llms Vllm — vLLM: high-throughput LLM serving, OpenAI API, quantization
pages.summary: This skill deploys production LLM APIs with vLLM, achieving 24x higher throughput than standard transformers through PagedAttention (block-based KV cache) and continuous batching. Supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints, quantization (GPTQ/AWQ/FP8), and tensor parallelism. Use for production inference, latency/throughput optimization, and serving models with limited GPU memory. Installation via pip install vllm. Basic usage includes offline inference and serving with vllm serve.

pages.title: Audiocraft Audio Generation — AudioCraft: MusicGen text-to-music, AudioGen text-to-sound
pages.summary: This skill uses Meta's AudioCraft for text-to-music and text-to-audio generation. Key components: MusicGen (text-to-music with melody conditioning), AudioGen (text-to-sound effects), and EnCodec (high-fidelity neural audio codec). Multiple model sizes from Small (300M) to Large (3.3B). Supports stereo output and style conditioning (MusicGen-Style). Use for music generation, sound effects, and building music generation applications. Alternatives: Stable Audio (longer commercial music), Bark (text-to-speech).

pages.summary: This skill uses Meta AI's Segment Anything Model (SAM) for zero-shot image segmentation. Works on any image domain without fine-tuning. Supports flexible prompts (points, bounding boxes, previous masks), automatic segmentation (all object masks), and high-quality masks trained on 1.1 billion masks from 11 million images. Multiple model sizes: ViT-B (fastest), ViT-L, ViT-H (most accurate). Supports ONNX export for browser and edge deployment. pages.title: Ocr And Documents — Extract text from PDFs/scans (pymupdf, marker-pdf)
pages.summary: This skill extracts text from PDFs and scanned documents. For DOCX use python-docx; for PPTX use powerpoint skill. For URLs, always try web_extract first. Local extraction: pymupdf (~25MB, fast, good for text-based PDFs with basic table support) vs marker-pdf (~3-5GB, slower, handles OCR, LaTeX, equations, code blocks, forms, headers/footers removal, reading order detection). Choose based on document type and quality needs.
pages.embedding_keywords: PDF extraction OCR pymupdf marker-pdf scanned document text extraction tables LaTeX web_extract
pages.prerequisites: Python; pip install pymupdf for basic; pip install marker-pdf for OCR (large download)

pages.title: Powerpoint — Create, read, edit .pptx decks, slides, notes, templates
pages.summary: This skill handles all .pptx file operations: creating slide decks, reading/parsing text, editing existing presentations, combining/splitting files, and working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, and comments. Triggered whenever user mentions deck, slides, presentation, or a .pptx filename. Reading uses python -m markitdown or thumbnail.py for visual overview. Editing workflow uses editing.md. Creating from scratch uses pptxgenjs.md. Also supports raw XML unpacking.
pages.embedding_keywords: PowerPoint pptx slide deck presentation markitdown thumbnail template speaker notes editing reading
pages.prerequisites: Python; markitdown, python-pptx, or pptxgenjs depending on operation

pages.title: Teams Meeting Pipeline
pages.summary: This skill operates the Teams meeting summary pipeline via Hermes CLI subcommands. Summarizes meetings, inspects pipeline status, replays jobs, and manages Microsoft Graph subscriptions. All operations are hermes teams-pipeline subcommands run via terminal. Covers extracting action items, checking stored jobs, replaying failed summaries, validating Graph setup, troubleshooting ingestion issues, and managing webhook subscriptions including automated renewal.
pages.embedding_keywords: Teams Microsoft Graph meeting summary transcript action items pipeline webhook subscription Hermes CLI
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Agent with Teams pipeline configured; Microsoft Graph API access

pages.title: Godmode — Jailbreak LLMs: Parseltongue, GODMODE, ULTRAPLINIAN
pages.summary: This skill bypasses safety filters on API-served LLMs using G0DM0D3 and L1B3RT4S techniques. Works at the prompt/API level on any accessible model including closed-source (GPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok). Three attack modes: GODMODE CLASSIC (system prompt templates), Parseltongue (encoding/obfuscation), and ULTRAPLINIAN (pre-fill engineering). Key difference from OBLITERATUS: this does not modify model weights. Can set up persistent jailbreaking in Hermes config.
pages.embedding_keywords: jailbreak G0DM0D3 L1B3RT4S Parseltongue GODMODE ULTRAPLINIAN safety bypass red-teaming prompt engineering LLM
pages.prerequisites: Access to target LLM API; Hermes Agent configured for jailbreak mode

pages.title: Arxiv — Search arXiv papers by keyword, author, category, or ID
pages.summary: This skill searches and retrieves academic papers from arXiv via their free REST API. No API key or dependencies required — just curl. Supports searching by keywords, getting specific papers by ID, reading abstracts via web_extract, and reading full PDFs. The API returns Atom XML which can be parsed with grep/sed or Python. Supports sorting by submitted date and filtering by category.
pages.embedding_keywords: arXiv academic papers research PDF abstract search REST API Atom XML citation science
pages.prerequisites: curl; optional Python for XML parsing

pages.title: Blogwatcher — Monitor blogs and RSS/Atom feeds via blogwatcher-cli tool
pages.summary: This skill tracks blog and RSS/Atom feed updates with the blogwatcher-cli tool. Supports automatic feed discovery, HTML scraping fallback, OPML import, and read/unread article management. Installation options: Go (go install), Docker, or pre-built binaries for Linux amd64/arm64 and macOS Apple Silicon/Intel. Uses a local database for storing feeds and articles.

pages.prerequisites: Go, Docker, or download pre-built binary; local storage for database

pages.title: Llm Wiki — Karpathy's LLM Wiki: build/query interlinked markdown KB
pages.summary: This skill builds and maintains a persistent, compounding knowledge base as interlinked markdown files based on Andrej Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern. Unlike traditional RAG, the wiki compiles knowledge once and keeps it current with cross-references and flagged contradictions. Human curates sources and directs analysis; agent summarizes, cross-references, files, and maintains consistency. Wiki location set via WIKI_PATH env var (default ~/wiki). Activates when user asks to create, ingest, query, or lint a wiki.
pages.embedding_keywords: LLM Wiki Karpathy knowledge base markdown interlinked RAG alternative cross-reference consistency research
pages.prerequisites: Directory for wiki storage; WIKI_PATH env var optional

pages.title: Polymarket — Query Polymarket: markets, prices, orderbooks, history
pages.summary: This skill queries prediction market data from Polymarket using their public REST APIs. All endpoints are read-only and require no authentication. Key concepts: Events contain Markets (1:many), markets are binary outcomes with Yes/No prices representing probabilities, volume is in USDC. Three APIs: Gamma API for discovery/search, CLOB API for real-time price/orderbook, and a history API for price trends. Use for checking odds, market prices, and prediction market movements.
pages.embedding_keywords: Polymarket prediction market odds probability USDC Gamma API CLOB orderbook binary outcome research
pages.prerequisites: None; all APIs are public and read-only

pages.title: Research Paper Writing — Write ML papers for NeurIPS/ICML/ICLR: design→submit
pages.summary: This skill provides an end-to-end pipeline for producing publication-ready ML/AI research papers targeting NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ACL, AAAI, and COLM. Covers full research lifecycle: experiment design, execution, monitoring, analysis, paper writing, review, revision, and submission. Not a linear pipeline — it is an iterative loop where results trigger new experiments and reviews trigger new analysis. Dependencies include semanticscholar, arxiv, habanero, scipy, numpy, matplotlib, and SciencePlots.
pages.embedding_keywords: research paper NeurIPS ICML ICLR ACL AAAI COLM ML experiment design LaTeX citation statistical analysis
code_snippets: pip install semanticscholar arxiv habanero requests scipy numpy matplotlib SciencePlots
pages.prerequisites: Python; dependencies installed; research topic and data

pages.title: Openhue — Control Philips Hue lights, scenes, rooms via OpenHue CLI
pages.summary: This skill controls Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI from the terminal. First run requires pressing the button on the Hue Bridge to pair (must be on same local network). Supports listing lights, rooms, and scenes; turning lights on/off; adjusting brightness (0-100); setting color temperature (153-500 mirek); and controlling specific rooms or individual bulbs. Install via pre-built binary (Linux) or Homebrew (macOS).

pages.title: Xurl — X/Twitter via xurl CLI: post, search, DM, media, v2 API
pages.summary: This skill uses xurl, the official X developer platform CLI for the X API v2. Supports posting, replying, quoting, deleting posts; searching posts and reading timelines/mentions; liking, reposting, bookmarking; following/unfollowing/blocking/muting; direct messages; media uploads (images and video); and raw access to any X API v2 endpoint. Replaces the older xitter skill. Supports OAuth 2.0 PKCE with auto-refresh. Critical security rules: never read ~/.xurl in LLM context, never ask for credentials in chat.

pages.title: Debugging Hermes Tui Commands — Debug Hermes TUI slash commands: Python, gateway, Ink UI
pages.summary: This skill debugs slash commands in the Hermes TUI which span three layers: Python command registry, tui_gateway JSON-RPC bridge, and Ink/TypeScript frontend. Bugs typically manifest as commands missing from autocomplete, working in CLI but not TUI, or config persisting but UI not updating. Covers architecture overview, layer synchronization, adding commands to both backend and frontend, and resolving inconsistencies between CLI and TUI behavior.
pages.embedding_keywords: Hermes TUI slash command debugging tui_gateway JSON-RPC Ink TypeScript Python autocomplete frontend backend
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Agent development environment; understanding of Python, TypeScript, and Ink

pages.title: Hermes Agent Skill Authoring — Author in-repo SKILL.md: frontmatter, validator, structure
pages.summary: This skill guides authoring Hermes-Agent skills that live in-repo (committed and shipped with the package) at /home/bb/hermes-agent/skills/<category>/<name>/SKILL.md. User-local skills live in ~/.hermes/skills/. In-repo skills require write_file + git add; skill_manage(action='create') does NOT target this tree. Covers required frontmatter format (starts with ---, version, author, license, platforms, tags), validation rules from skill_manager_tool.py, and when to use in-repo vs user-local skills.
pages.embedding_keywords: SKILL.md skill authoring frontmatter in-repo validation Hermes Agent git frontmatter format development
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Agent repo access; understanding of SKILL.md frontmatter format

pages.title: Node Inspect Debugger — Debug Node.js via --inspect + Chrome DevTools Protocol CLI
pages.summary: This skill drives Node's built-in V8 inspector programmatically from the terminal for real breakpoints, step in/over/out, call-stack walking, scope dumps, and expression evaluation. Two tools: node inspect (built-in, zero install, CLI REPL, best for quick poking) and ndb/CDP via chrome-remote-interface (scriptable from Node/Python, best for automated breakpoints and non-interactive debugging). Prefer node inspect first. Use for failing tests, ui-tui crashes, tui_gateway child processes, and closure inspection.
pages.embedding_keywords: Node.js debugging V8 inspector node inspect ndb Chrome DevTools Protocol CDP breakpoint scope CLI
pages.prerequisites: Node.js installed; optional ndb or chrome-remote-interface for advanced debugging

pages.title: Plan — Plan mode: write markdown plan to .hermes/plans/, no exec
pages.summary: This skill activates plan mode where the agent plans only without implementing code, editing project files (except the plan markdown), or running mutating terminal commands. Deliverable is a concrete markdown plan saved under .hermes/plans/YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSS-<slug>.md. Plan includes goal, current context, proposed approach, step-by-step plan, files likely to change, tests/validation, and risks/tradeoffs. Use when the user wants a plan instead of execution.
pages.embedding_keywords: plan mode markdown planning .hermes/plans goal approach step-by-step risks validation software development
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Agent with plan mode support

pages.title: Python Debugpy — Debug Python: pdb REPL + debugpy remote (DAP)
pages.summary: This skill provides three Python debugging tools selected by situation: breakpoint() + pdb for local interactive debugging (simplest, add breakpoint() in source), python -m pdb for launching existing scripts without edits, and debugpy for remote/headless/attach-to-running-process scenarios (talks DAP, scriptable from terminal, works for long-lived processes like gateway and daemon). Start with breakpoint() as the cheapest option. Use for test failures, long-running processes, post-mortem crashes, and subprocess inspection.
pages.embedding_keywords: Python debugging pdb debugpy breakpoint DAP remote debugging post-mortem REPL long-lived process
pages.prerequisites: Python installed; debugpy for remote debugging (pip install debugpy)

pages.title: Requesting Code Review — Pre-commit review: security scan, quality gates, auto-fix
pages.summary: This skill provides an automated verification pipeline before code lands: static scans, baseline-aware quality gates, an independent reviewer subagent, and an auto-fix loop. Core principle: no agent should verify its own work — fresh context finds what you miss. Use after implementing a feature or bug fix, before git commit or push, or after 2+ file edits. Skip for documentation-only changes, pure config tweaks, or when user says 'skip verification'. Differs from github-code-review which reviews OTHER people's PRs.
pages.embedding_keywords: code review pre-commit security scan quality gate auto-fix static analysis verification subagent
pages.prerequisites: Git repository; Hermes Agent with subagent support

pages.title: Spike — Throwaway experiments to validate an idea before build
pages.summary: This skill validates ideas through disposable throwaway experiments before committing to real builds. Use when the user wants to 'feel out an idea', validate feasibility, compare approaches, or surface unknowns. Spikes are disposable by design — throw them away once they've paid their debt. Load when user says 'let me try this', 'spike this out', 'is this even possible?', or 'compare A vs B'. Not for knowable-from-docs answers, production path work, or already-validated ideas. Prefer gsd-spike if available.
pages.embedding_keywords: spike throwaway experiment feasibility prototype proof of concept validation exploration MVP software development

pages.title: Subagent Driven Development — Execute plans via delegate_task subagents (2-stage review)
pages.summary: This skill executes implementation plans by dispatching fresh subagents per task with systematic two-stage review. Core principle: fresh subagent per task plus two-stage review (spec then quality) equals high quality and fast iteration. Use when you have an implementation plan, tasks are mostly independent, quality matters, and you want automated review. Benefits: fresh context per task, automated review catches issues early, consistent quality checks, subagents can ask questions. Process: read plan, extract all tasks, create todo list, dispatch subagents, review output.
pages.embedding_keywords: subagent driven development delegate task two-stage review implementation plan dispatch quality gate spec
pages.prerequisites: Hermes Agent with delegate_task support; implementation plan available

pages.title: Systematic Debugging — 4-phase root cause debugging: understand bugs before fixing
pages.summary: This skill enforces a 4-phase systematic debugging process: understand, reproduce, isolate, fix. Core principle: ALWAYS find root cause before attempting fixes — symptom fixes are failure. The Iron Law: no fixes without root cause investigation first. If Phase 1 is incomplete, cannot propose fixes. Use for ANY technical issue: test failures, production bugs, unexpected behavior, performance problems, build failures, integration issues. Especially critical under time pressure or when 'just one quick fix' seems obvious.
pages.embedding_keywords: systematic debugging root cause 4-phase understand reproduce isolate fix Iron Law bug troubleshooting

pages.title: Test Driven Development — TDD: enforce RED-GREEN-REFACTOR, tests before code
pages.summary: This skill enforces Test-Driven Development with the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle. Core principle: if you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing. The Iron Law: no production code without a failing test first — write code before the test, delete it and start over. Use always for new features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes. Exceptions (ask user first): throwaway prototypes, generated code, configuration files. RED: write minimal failing test. GREEN: write minimal code to pass. REFACTOR: clean up while keeping tests green.
pages.embedding_keywords: TDD test driven development RED GREEN REFACTOR unit test failing test refactoring behavior verification
pages.prerequisites: Testing framework installed (pytest, unittest, etc.); understanding of unit tests

pages.title: Writing Plans — Write implementation plans: bite-sized tasks, paths, code
pages.summary: This skill writes comprehensive implementation plans assuming the implementer has zero context and questionable taste. Documents everything needed: files to touch, complete code, testing commands, docs to check, verification steps. Gives bite-sized tasks following DRY, YAGNI, TDD, and frequent commits. Core principle: a good plan makes implementation obvious — if someone has to guess, the plan is incomplete. Use always before multi-step features, complex requirements, or delegating to subagents. Don't skip even for simple features or solo work.
pages.embedding_keywords: implementation plan writing plans bite-sized tasks DRY YAGNI TDD file paths testing verification delegation
pages.prerequisites: Understanding of codebase; ability to identify files and dependencies

pages.title: Yuanbao — Yuanbao (元宝) groups: @mention users, query info/members
pages.summary: This skill handles Yuanbao (元宝) group interactions. The agent's text reply is automatically sent to the group — no special send tool needed. Including @nickname in reply text automatically converts to a real @mention that notifies the user. Available tools: yb_query_group_info (group name, owner, member count), yb_query_group_members (find user, list bots, list all members, get nickname for @mention), and yb_send_dm (send private/direct message with optional media).
